system_pipeline.description: The system records cough audio using a microphone or accepts uploaded audio files.
Noise reduction, normalization, and voice activity detection are applied to isolate cough segments.
Multiple acoustic features are extracted including Mel spectrograms, MFCCs, RMS energy, and spectral descriptors to represent cough characteristics.
Temporal-frequency features and statistical descriptors are combined to create a robust representation of cough audio.
A hybrid CNN-Dense neural network analyzes features to classify respiratory conditions.
A secondary classifier identifies fake or non-genuine cough sounds.
The system returns predicted disease category with confidence scores.
